metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_431
metadata.comment:
Rationale: CPEB3 is strongly enriched in the PSD fraction of mouse brain. In addition, the protein is detected by immunofluorescence in DIV21 hippocampal neurons, where it is juxtaposed to synaptophysin (Fig. 5A), which is taken to indicate a pre-synaptic localisation. Since, however, the optical resolution in the immunofluorescence is not sufficient to decide if the in-vitro staining is pre- or post-synaptic, there seems to be more evidence for a post-synaptic localisation.

This annotation is also supported by Pavlopoulos et al., Cell 2011.

24/9/2017 Pim
- Literal: "Finally, both CPEB3 and CPEB4, when fused to GFP, were detected in dendrites, often as puncta (Figure 5C)"
- Literal: "Taken together, these data show that CPEB3 and CPEB4 are expressed in only partially overlapping regions of the brain; within hippocampal neurons, where both are expressed, they appear to be synaptic."
- The mRNA translation data is too poorly shown in this paper lacking clear synaptic relevance although looking at AMPAR translation.. Experimental description: Hippocampal neurons were cultured and immunostained as described (Huang et al, 2002): no indication of species.

24/9/2017 Pim
- Information on tissues and OE constructs used in Fig.5 are not stated by the authors. Authors contacted in email dd. 24/9/2017 (Joel.Richter@umassmed.edu). Answer:

"rat" cultured neurons.
GFP-hCPEB3 and GFP-hCPEB4 (human).
Thank, Yi-Shuian
- Rat species was selected based on the localization in the PSD fraction.
